title: News sites are the new newspapers: People are abandoning them for social media
tldr: Across all age groups worldwide, audiences are ditching publishers' sites and apps for social and video platforms, with under-35s leading the exodus. A structural shift that reshapes where content distribution actually happens.
why_fresh: data confirming a structural distribution shift
angle: Why owned media destinations are losing relevance and what 'distribution-first' content strategy looks like now.

title: Your Best-Ranked Page Might Be Invisible to Google's AI
tldr: Pages ranking in Google's top 10 are often absent from AI Overviews citations, decoupling traditional SEO rank from AI visibility. A fundamental rethink for anyone optimizing content for discovery.
why_fresh: new gap between SEO rank and AI citation
angle: How content strategists should redesign pages to be cited by AI, not just ranked by search.

title: When everyone is a thought leader
tldr: AI makes thought leadership trivially easy to produce, so standing out now requires proof, risk, and lived experience that can't be copied. A sharp framing of the post-AI authority problem.
why_fresh: reframes thought leadership in AI saturation
angle: The new currency of thought leadership: differentiated proof and point of view in an AI-flooded feed.

title: LinkedIn's Algorithm Killed Company Pages. Employees Are The Distribution Now.
tldr: LinkedIn's algorithm has gutted company page reach, pushing brands to rely on employees as the real distribution channel. Direct implications for employee-generated content and internal comms.
angle: Building an employee-advocacy distribution engine now that brand pages are dead on arrival.

title: Substack is launching a sponsorship program
tldr: Substack rolled out native brand sponsorships plus 'Creator Kits' to help writers build media kits, hiring its first head of brand sponsorships. A meaningful move in newsletter monetization.
angle: What Substack's sponsorship play means for newsletter strategy and the brand-creator economy.

title: The #1 Role Your Content Team Needs in 2026 Is a Managing Editor
tldr: As AI makes content cheap to produce, editorial judgment becomes the differentiator, making a managing editor the most valuable hire. A clear argument for human governance over content volume.
why_fresh: reframes team structure for AI era
angle: Why editorial judgment is the scarce resource in an AI content economy.

title: The Scoop: The rise of 'clipping' on social media
tldr: 'Clipping'—cutting long videos into short shareable segments—is becoming a core PR tactic, with new disclosure risks attached. A practical short-form trend reshaping earned media.
angle: How clipping turns one asset into many—and the governance risks PR teams ignore.

title: Building a tier-1 media map for new media
tldr: With 17,000+ media jobs cut in 2025, tier-1 outlets no longer mean what they used to; PR pros must remap influence across newsletters, creators and niche platforms. A timely media relations rethink.
angle: How to rebuild your media map when legacy tier-1 outlets lose their reach.

title: The Future of Content Belongs to the Tastemakers
tldr: With AI making polished copy effortless and forgettable, the differentiator becomes curation and taste. A strong thesis on what survives content commoditization.
angle: Curation and taste as the last defensible content skill when AI writes everything.

title: The 4 PR and Marketing Metrics that Matter for AI Visibility
tldr: Gini Dietrich argues impressions, reach and AVEs won't survive scrutiny, naming four metrics that actually prove AI visibility. A concrete measurement update for the AI search era.
why_fresh: new measurement framework for AI visibility
angle: Retiring vanity metrics: measuring share of voice inside AI answers.

title: Why 'AI Productivity Gains' Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Pitching AI as productivity gains fails to move CMOs, CFOs and legal; the metric must be reframed per audience to defend budget and headcount. Practical stakeholder communication tactics.
angle: How to translate AI value into the language each C-suite stakeholder actually rewards.

title: OpenAI moves to automate ad creative
tldr: OpenAI is moving to automate the creative production advertisers still do by hand, signaling a major shift in who builds ad content. A direct threat-and-opportunity for content and creative teams.
angle: What happens to creative craft and brand consistency when AI generates the ads.

title: The Anti-AI Brand Is Becoming a Real Market Position
tldr: Rejecting AI is emerging as a deliberate brand positioning strategy, signaling human craft and authenticity. A fresh angle on brand differentiation amid AI saturation.
angle: Is 'human-made' the next premium brand promise? The rise of anti-AI positioning.

title: Adapting misinformation strategy for the AI age
tldr: AI didn't create misinformation but made it faster and harder to correct; the American Academy of Family Physicians shows what a credible response now requires. A useful crisis/issue management case.
angle: Building a rapid-correction playbook for AI-accelerated misinformation.

title: Плохие привычки руководителей в 2026 году и как их избежать
tldr: SETTERS examines eight key mistakes modern leaders make, from micromanaging steps to failing to manage by results. A practical leadership and internal-comms read for a Russian-speaking audience.
angle: Managing outcomes vs. activity: leadership habits that shape internal communication culture.
